cannot_score_reason: Video content does not contain an actual performance of FLS Peg Transfer task. Content shows: (1) trophy/award display, (2) facility tours and educational demonstrations, (3) equipment handling by multiple instructors, (4) teaching laboratory environment with groups of students/trainees observing, (5) award ceremony/recognition event. No single trainee is shown performing the complete peg transfer task sequence (transferring 6 objects from non-dominant to dominant side, then returning them) from start to completion with observable timing.

video_segments_identified.content: Trophy/award display labeled 'TOP GUN Champion', medical facility background
Mobile device and equipment handling, appears to be scoring or timing device used in training environment
Group of trainees and instructors in simulation lab observing demonstration or training session
Monitor displays showing various simulator/training interface screens (not peg transfer task execution)
Hands-on demonstration with instruments and equipment in training context; multiple observers present
Detailed view of peg transfer board setup with pegs and objects on monitor display; no live task performance visible
Trainee performing peg transfer task at laparoscopic simulator with audience observing; camera angle does not capture clear view of object transfers
Close-up of hand holding timing/scoring device during task execution
Award presentation; trainee holding 'TOP GUN' award trophy after task completion

observability_assessment: While frames 29-32 show a trainee at the simulator workstation with what appears to be a peg transfer board visible, the video quality, camera angle, and presence of multiple observers (instructional/demonstration context) prevent reliable extraction of: (1) exact completion time, (2) object drop events or penalties, (3) phase timing boundaries, (4) hand dominance and transfer sequence validation. The video is primarily an instructional or celebratory recording of a training event rather than an isolated performance recording suitable for official FLS scoring.
recommendation: This video is classified as instructional/celebratory documentation of an FLS training program and competition event. It is not suitable for formal FLS task scoring. To score this performance, request the isolated camera feed showing only the pegboard from the overhead perspective, with clear visibility of all object transfers from start (first touch) to end (final release on original side).
confidence_rationale: High confidence that this is not a scorable performance video based on clear contextual clues: trophy display, award ceremony setting, multiple instructors/observers, facility tour footage, and lack of clean isolated task execution footage. The few seconds showing task performance are insufficient and poorly positioned for reliable scoring.
